summary: Iran launched missiles and drones targeting U.S. forces in Bahrain and Kuwait, prompting U.S. retaliatory strikes on Iranian positions near Qeshm Island. Hezbollah rejected a comprehensive ceasefire in southern Lebanon, and the IDF continued operations against Hezbollah infrastructure while issuing evacuation warnings for Nabatieh. The IDF also killed five Hamas terrorists in Gaza involved in October 7th kidnapping operations and arrested a Hamas militant holding the body of fallen soldier Oron Shaul.
key_points: Iran launched missiles and drones at U.S. forces in Bahrain and Kuwait; U.S. retaliated by striking Iranian positions near Qeshm Island (Source: @pkpoi/142395).
Hezbollah demands a comprehensive ceasefire across all of southern Lebanon, not limited to the Dahiyeh area (Source: @New_security8200/200886).
IDF Division 91 and the Air Force struck Hezbollah weapons storage facilities, command posts, and terror infrastructure in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24105).
IDF issued evacuation warnings for the southern Lebanon city of Nabatieh ahead of planned strikes targeting Hezbollah (Source: @manniefabian/58377).
IDF killed five Hamas terrorists in Gaza who advanced terror targeting against IDF forces, including operatives involved in October 7th kidnappings (Source: @New_security8200/200869).
IDF and Shin Bet arrested Hamas militant Ibrahim Khilo in Gaza, who held the body of fallen soldier Oron Shaul (Source: @pkpoi/142388).
Iranian attacks on Kuwait International Airport caused injuries and serious damage to several facilities; Bahrain also activated emergency sirens (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365312).
The fourth meeting between Israeli and Lebanese representatives took place in Washington (Source: @amitsegal/61668).

sections.executive_summary: Iran launched a missile and drone attack targeting U.S. military forces stationed in Bahrain and Kuwait, marking a significant escalation in direct Iran-U.S. hostilities. The United States responded with retaliatory strikes on an Iranian military position near Qeshm Island. Concurrently, Hezbollah rejected ceasefire terms in Lebanon, and the IDF intensified operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon and Hamas in Gaza.

points: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps launched a large-scale missile and drone attack targeting U.S. military bases in Kuwait and the U.S. 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, according to reports from Iranian-aligned sources (Source: @lelotsenzura/92453).
The attacks on Kuwait International Airport reportedly caused injuries and serious damage to several airport facilities, with Kuwait activating its emergency plan (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365312).

The U.S. Central Command denied Iranian claims of successful strikes, stating that all attacks on U.S. forces in Bahrain and Kuwait were defeated and that several drones were successfully intercepted (Source: @VahidOnline/75889).
The U.S. retaliated by striking an Iranian military position near Qeshm Island in response to the attacks on its forces in the Gulf (Source: @pkpoi/142395).
Kuwait's Council of Ministers demanded Iran immediately and unconditionally cease its aggressions and held Iran fully responsible for the attacks (Source: @shin_persian/6439).

Iranian authorities reported controlled explosions in the Baharistan area south of Isfahan between 8-10 AM on June 13 (Khordad), stating there was no cause for public concern (Source: @SharghDaily/163553).

The IDF continued operations in southern Lebanon, with Division 91 and the Air Force striking Hezbollah weapons storage facilities, command posts, and additional terror infrastructure from which Hezbollah operatives were active (Source: @idf_telegram/24105).
The IDF reiterated its evacuation warning for the southern Lebanon city of Nabatieh ahead of planned strikes targeting Hezbollah positions (Source: @manniefabian/58377).

An IDF reservist was moderately injured and three other soldiers were lightly injured by an explosive-laden drone strike in southern Lebanon; all were evacuated to hospital (Source: @almogboker78/17285).
The IDF and Shin Bet conducted a secret operation in Gaza arresting Hamas militant Ibrahim Khilo of the Shati Battalion, who was holding the body of fallen soldier Oron Shaul (Source: @pkpoi/142388).

IDF forces killed five Hamas terrorists in Gaza who had advanced terror targeting against IDF forces, including operatives involved in explosive preparation and sniper targeting, some linked to October 7th kidnappings (Source: @New_security8200/200869).

IDF reservist unit 'Duvdevan' arrested a three-cell terrorist squad in the Menashe Brigade area that was organizing to carry out an attack, uncovering a concealed improvised 'Carlo' weapon (Source: @idf_telegram/24104).
The fourth meeting between Israeli and Lebanese representatives took place in Washington (Source: @amitsegal/61668).
IDF Head of Operations Brigadier General Itzik Cohen accepted the request of Operations Division Head Brig. Gen. Israel Shomer to retire immediately for personal reasons (Source: @pkpoi/142393).

The Home Front Command updated defense policy for northern Israel, moving several communities from reduced to partial activity levels while lifting restrictions in Upper Galilee and northern Golan areas (Source: @idf_telegram/24107).

IDF Division 252 forces in the Southern Command killed five Hamas terrorists in Gaza who advanced terror targeting against IDF forces, including a Hamas operative from the Shuja'iyya Battalion involved in explosive preparation (Source: @New_security8200/200869).

Among those killed was Said Faisal Said Shamali, a Nukhba squad commander in Hamas, and Ahmad Ali Abd al-Rahim Halas, a sniper squad commander in the Palestinian Islamic Jihad (Source: @New_security8200/200869).
The IDF and Shin Bet arrested Hamas militant Ibrahim Khilo of the Shati Battalion in a secret operation in Gaza; he was found holding the body of fallen soldier Oron Shaul (Source: @pkpoi/142388).
An indictment was filed against Ibrahim Khilo, with a prosecutor's statement expected to be submitted to the Be'er Sheva District Court on Thursday (Source: @pkpoi/142388).

The IDF killed a Hamas militant who participated in the kidnapping of Hersh Goldberg-Polin, Eli-ah Cohen, Alon Ahal, and Or Levi from the Re'im junction during the October 7th massacre (Source: @New_security8200/200882).
Hezbollah demanded a comprehensive ceasefire across all of southern Lebanon, going beyond the condition that the IDF not attack the Dahiyeh area (Source: @New_security8200/200886).
The IDF reiterated evacuation warnings for Nabatieh city in southern Lebanon ahead of planned strikes targeting Hezbollah (Source: @manniefabian/58377).
IDF Division 91 and the Air Force struck Hezbollah weapons storage facilities, command posts, and terror infrastructure in southern Lebanon (Source: @almogboker78/17278).

An IDF reservist was moderately injured and three additional soldiers lightly injured by an explosive-laden drone in southern Lebanon; all were evacuated for medical treatment (Source: @almogboker78/17285).

Israeli officials reported that Hezbollah has not stopped fighting despite Trump's pledge for a ceasefire, and Hezbollah publicly rejects the equation of a ceasefire in the Dahiyeh in exchange for a ceasefire in northern Israel (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365318).
The fourth round of talks between Israeli and Lebanese representatives took place in Washington (Source: @amitsegal/61668).
Reports indicate drone strikes hit the Arzon and Deir al-Zahrani areas in Lebanon (Source: @redlinkleb/20525, @redlinkleb/20536).
Military aircraft were reported in the skies over Kirkuk province in northern Iraq (Source: @naya_foriraq/76836).

Iran launched missiles and drones targeting U.S. forces at the 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain and military installations in Kuwait; Bahrain's Ministry of Interior confirmed sirens sounded due to hostile missile and drone attacks (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3290).

Kuwait International Airport was targeted by Iranian missiles and drones, resulting in injuries and serious damage to several airport facilities; Kuwait activated its emergency plan (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365312).

The U.S. Central Command denied Iranian claims of successful strikes on U.S. forces in Bahrain and Kuwait, stating all attacks were defeated and several drones were intercepted with no U.S. personnel or equipment damaged (Source: @VahidOnline/75889).
Kuwait's Council of Ministers demanded Iran immediately and unconditionally cease its aggressions and held Iran fully responsible (Source: @shin_persian/6439).
Bahraini authorities conducted raids across various areas of Bahrain and arrested several Shia followers of Ahl al-Bayt in connection with the Iranian attacks (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365323).
U.S. CENTCOM reported disabling an oil tanker that was heading toward Iran (Source: @rodast_omiddana/72447).
Defense systems in Kuwait were activated in response to missile and drone launches (Source: @New_security8200/200890).

key_developments: Iran launched a large-scale missile and drone attack targeting U.S. forces at the 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain and U.S. military bases in Kuwait, with Iranian-aligned sources claiming the strikes were in response to a U.S. attack near Qeshm Island (Source: @lelotsenzura/92453).

The United States retaliated against Iran by striking an Iranian military position near Qeshm Island following the Iranian missile and drone attacks on U.S. forces in the Gulf (Source: @pkpoi/142395).

Kuwait International Airport sustained injuries and serious facility damage from Iranian missile and drone strikes, prompting activation of Kuwait's emergency plan and a formal demand for Iran to cease attacks unconditionally (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365312).

Hezbollah publicly rejected a comprehensive ceasefire in southern Lebanon and refused the equation of a Dahiyeh ceasefire in exchange for a northern Israel ceasefire, while the IDF continued striking Hezbollah infrastructure (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/365318).

The IDF killed five Hamas terrorists in Gaza involved in October 7th kidnapping operations and arrested a Hamas militant holding the body of fallen soldier Oron Shaul in a secret operation (Source: @New_security8200/200869, @pkpoi/142388).

IDF Division 91 and the Air Force continued sustained strikes on Hezbollah weapons storage facilities, command posts, and terror infrastructure in southern Lebanon, with the IDF issuing evacuation warnings for Nabatieh (Source: @idf_telegram/24105, @manniefabian/58377).

The fourth round of Israeli-Lebanese negotiations took place in Washington amid ongoing hostilities, with Israeli officials noting Hezbollah has not ceased fire despite diplomatic efforts (Source: @amitsegal/61668).
sections.threat_assessment: The Middle East is experiencing a significant escalation as Iran directly targeted U.S. military forces in Bahrain and Kuwait with missiles and drones, prompting U.S. retaliatory strikes near Qeshm Island — marking a dangerous broadening of direct Iran-U.S. hostilities beyond proxy engagements. Hezbollah's rejection of ceasefire terms and continued operations in southern Lebanon, combined with the IDF's sustained strikes and evacuation warnings for Nabatieh, indicate the Lebanon front is intensifying rather than de-escalating. The attacks on Kuwait International Airport represent a notable escalation targeting civilian infrastructure in a Gulf state, raising the risk of further regional widening. The arrest of a Hamas militant holding the body of a fallen Israeli soldier and the killing of October 7th-linked operatives in Gaza underscore ongoing counter-terrorism operations across multiple fronts simultaneously.
sections.regional_response: Kuwait's Council of Ministers issued a formal demand for Iran to immediately and unconditionally cease its aggressions, holding Iran fully responsible for the attacks on its territory and civilian infrastructure. The U.S. Central Command publicly denied Iranian claims of successful strikes, asserting that all attacks were defeated and that U.S. air defense successfully intercepted several drones with no personnel or equipment losses. Bahrain activated its emergency sirens and conducted domestic raids, arresting Shia individuals in connection with the Iranian attacks. The United States maintained its commitment to the ceasefire with Iran despite the exchange of strikes, even as it disabled an oil tanker heading toward Iran.
sections.intelligence_notes: The exchange of direct fire between Iran and the United States — Iranian strikes on U.S. forces in Bahrain and Kuwait followed by U.S. retaliation near Qeshm Island — represents a significant escalation pattern that risks further tit-for-tat cycles. Hezbollah's public rejection of ceasefire terms and Israel's report that Hezbollah continued fighting despite Trump's ceasefire pledge suggest diplomatic efforts in Washington are not translating into operational de-escalation on the ground. The targeting of Kuwait International Airport, a civilian facility, marks an escalation in Iranian targeting norms within the Gulf and may prompt stronger coalition responses. Multiple fronts remain simultaneously active — Iran-Gulf, Israel-Lebanon, and Israel-Gaza — indicating a highly complex and interconnected threat environment with significant potential for further escalation.
